raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
The full report shows a favourable variance of £7,966 (+1,373.4%) against a budget of £580, driven almost entirely by a Sales outperformance of £5,121 (+1,024.1%). Three Q4 2025 deals (Redwood Group, Apex Analytics, and Fortis Ltd) slipped past the December close and were recognised in January, a variance confirmed as expected and approved by the CFO. The remaining report variance sits across insignificant lines detailed at the foot of this report.

LINE COMMENTARY

Sales | +£5,121 (+1024.1%) | Budget: £500 | Actual: £5,621
Three Q4 2025 deals that did not close before year-end — Redwood Group (£2,100 ARR), Apex Analytics (£1,850 ARR), and Fortis Ltd (£1,300 ARR) — all signed in early January, with revenue recognition approved by the CFO; the January budget of £500 had been set assuming a quiet month, making the favourable variance expected rather than structural. The CRM carry-over total of £5,250 is broadly consistent with the £5,121 recognised in the period, with the residual difference likely attributable to first-month billing timing (Slack cites £3,200 combined first-month billing versus full ARR), though this has not been largely reconciled to the ledger and may warrant a brief confirmation with Finance.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:15:00Z - crm-sales-broad - crm-q4-carryover: CRM pipeline report January 2026. Redwood Group — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £2,100). Apex Analytics — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £1,850). Fortis Ltd — Closed Won 9 Jan (ARR £1,300). Total carry-over from Q4 2025: £5,250.
- Gmail - 2024-11-05T18:15:00Z - gmail-sales-broad - gmail-2026-01-08-pipeline: Q4 pipeline carry-over memo from CEO — three major deals (Redwood, Apex, Fortis) that were forecast to close by 31 December 2025 have now signed in January. Revenue recognition approved by CFO. January budget variance is expected and approved.
- Slack - 2024-11-05T12:15:00Z - slack-sales-broad - sales-slack-2026-01-08: Big news — three Q4 deals that didn't close before Christmas have landed in January. Redwood, Apex, and Fortis all signed this week. Combined first-month billing £3,200. The January budget was set at £500 assuming a quiet month — looks like the pipeline catch-up exceeded expectations.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines are below the significance threshold and require no further commentary at this time: Audit & Accountancy Fees (+£47), Bank Fees (+£15), Entertainment — 100% Business (+£13), General Expenses (+£308), Light Power & Heating (+£49), Motor Vehicle Expenses (+£124), Printing & Stationery (+£18), Rent (+£984), Subscriptions (+£1,220), Telephone & Internet (+£37), and Travel National (+£29).
